rubric_items.reasoning: The agent correctly distinguished the CockroachDB 'cluster setting version' (the feature-gate version, i.e. what features are unlocked cluster-wide) from the binary version, and recorded the former (24.1) as db_version — which is the semantically correct answer to 'which feature set is the cluster operating under'. It also checked preserve_downgrade_option to confirm the cluster had fully finalized upgrades, showing real understanding rather than a guess. The ConfigMap is a durable, standard K8s object that will survive restarts and re-checks. Oracle passed, confirming correctness.

The agent gathered all three diagnostic facts (cluster setting version, binary version, preserve_downgrade_option) up front in one batched command, reasoned about which value was the right one to record, checked whether the ConfigMap already existed before acting, and then created it — a clean diagnose-then-act sequence with no backtracking or wasted turns (4 total turns).

Used read-only kubectl exec/get calls for diagnosis and a single scoped kubectl create configmap --from-literal for the mutation — least-privilege, targeted commands with no broad or risky flags, matching the API activity log (1 mutation, 11 reads).
Exactly one mutation (creating the ConfigMap with the single required key), no unrelated changes or collateral churn — the minimal possible solution to the stated task.
